metadata.note: All 3 evals run via live delegation subagent executor + main-agent grader. No analytical stubs.

expectations.text: Response provides the correct Sefaria endpoint URL with URL-encoded reference
expectations.evidence: 'GET https://www.sefaria.org/api/texts/Psalms%2023:1?lang=en' -- %20 encoding, correct lang param

expectations.text: Response explains the text normalization steps (strip HTML, flatten array)
expectations.evidence: Three-step normalization: flatten arrays, strip HTML via regex, trim whitespace

expectations.text: Response shows how to extract text from the json.text field
expectations.evidence: Code uses data.text -- consistent with skill's Sefaria response documentation

expectations.text: Response mentions the Sefaria attribution requirement (CC BY-SA 2.0)
expectations.evidence: 'Sefaria.org -- CC BY-SA 2.0' attribution requirement specified

expectations.text: Response provides the correct Sefaria endpoint URL with URL-encoded reference
expectations.evidence: Uses Psalms.23.1 dot notation -- valid alternative Sefaria format, functionally correct

expectations.text: Response explains the text normalization steps (strip HTML, flatten array)
expectations.evidence: Array.isArray() + join() + regex strip -- both normalization steps covered

expectations.text: Response shows how to extract text from the json.text field
expectations.evidence: Used data.en, not data.text. ARE standard is data.text.

expectations.text: Response mentions the Sefaria attribution requirement (CC BY-SA 2.0)
expectations.evidence: No attribution mentioned -- legal compliance gap

expectations.text: Response provides the correct bible-api.com endpoint with translation=web parameter
expectations.evidence: 'GET https://bible-api.com/john%203:16?translation=web' -- correct endpoint

expectations.text: Response identifies WEB as including deuterocanonicals
expectations.evidence: 'includes deuterocanonical books (Tobit, Judith, 1-2 Maccabees, Wisdom, Sirach, Baruch)'

expectations.text: Response notes WEB is best for Catholic and Orthodox denominations
expectations.evidence: Catholic and Orthodox both identified as primary targets for WEB

expectations.text: Response shows json.text extraction with whitespace normalization
expectations.evidence: Shows data.text access but not the whitespace normalization (.replace('\n', ' '))

expectations.text: Response provides the correct bible-api.com endpoint with translation=web parameter
expectations.evidence: 'https://bible-api.com/john+3:16?translation=web' -- + encoding, translation=web correct

expectations.text: Response identifies WEB as including deuterocanonicals
expectations.evidence: CRITICAL ERROR: says WEB 'typically lacks the Deuterocanon/Apocrypha in its standard form' -- the OPPOSITE of the truth. Would cause developer to select wrong translation for Catholic users.

expectations.text: Response notes WEB is best for Catholic and Orthodox denominations
expectations.evidence: Focuses on Protestant/Messianic/missions use cases. Catholic/Orthodox not mentioned.

expectations.text: Response shows json.text extraction with whitespace normalization
expectations.evidence: Shows console.log(data) -- prints entire object, not data.text extraction

expectations.text: Response correctly identifies D&C 76:22 as a non-Bible LDS reference
expectations.evidence: 'isLdsBibleRef returns true only for 66 books of the Protestant Bible. D&C is not a Bible book, returns false.'

expectations.text: Response explains isLdsBibleRef returns false for this reference
expectations.evidence: Explicitly stated with routing code: isLdsBibleRef → false → fetchNephiPassage

expectations.text: Response identifies scriptures.nephi.org as the routing target
expectations.evidence: 'routes the request to scriptures.nephi.org... path becomes /verses/D%26C%2076%3A22'

expectations.text: Response mentions LdsApiUnavailableError and the static fallback message
expectations.evidence: LdsApiUnavailableError named and exact fallback message directing to churchofjesuschrist.org/study/scriptures quoted

expectations.text: Response correctly identifies D&C 76:22 as a non-Bible LDS reference
expectations.evidence: 'identifies book abbreviation D&C... flags for specialized handling... does not go to bible-api.com'

expectations.text: Response explains isLdsBibleRef returns false for this reference
expectations.evidence: Never mentions isLdsBibleRef

expectations.text: Response identifies scriptures.nephi.org as the routing target
expectations.evidence: Mentions src/api/nephi.ts as hint but not scriptures.nephi.org URL. Suggests 'custom LDS API' vaguely.

expectations.text: Response mentions LdsApiUnavailableError and the static fallback message
expectations.evidence: No error type or fallback message -- unhandled rejection on API failure

notes: All 3 evals: LIVE delegation subagent runs
Eval 2 with_skill 0.75: whitespace normalization missing from code example -- should be added to skill as explicit .replace pattern
Eval 2 without_skill CRITICAL ERROR: said WEB 'lacks deuterocanon' -- WRONG, WEB includes deuterocanon on bible-api.com. Would cause Catholic user to get wrong translation.
Eval 3 without_skill: correctly identified D&C=LDS but no isLdsBibleRef, no scriptures.nephi.org, no error handling
